name: when-releasing-software-use-github-release-management
description: Comprehensive GitHub release orchestration with AI swarm coordination for automated versioning, testing, deployment, and rollback management. Coordinates release-manager, cicd-engineer, tester, and docs-writer agents through hierarchical topology to handle semantic versioning, changelog generation, release notes, deployment validation, and post-release monitoring. Supports multiple release strategies (rolling, blue-green, canary) and automated rollback. Use when creating releases, managing deployments, or coordinating version updates.
capabilities: Coordinate hierarchical AI agent swarm for release operations
Determine version bumps using semantic versioning rules
Execute multi-phase release testing and validation
Generate changelogs and release notes automatically
Deploy using rolling, blue-green, or canary strategies
Trigger automated rollback on deployment failures
author: DNYoussef
securitySummary: This skill contains only documentation and workflow descriptions - no executable code exists. All referenced scripts (semver.sh, deploy.sh, etc.) are documentation placeholders. Static analysis flagged patterns like shell backticks and cryptographic algorithms because it cannot distinguish between example documentation and actual code. The skill-report.json confirms safe: risk_level is 'safe', is_blocked is false, and safe_to_publish is true. This is a pure prompt engineering skill.
valueStatement: Software releases require coordinating version bumps, testing, deployment, and documentation. This skill orchestrates multiple AI agents to handle the entire release workflow automatically. It ensures consistent processes, reduces human error, and provides rollback capabilities.
userTitle: Automate software releases with AI agents
limitations: Requires existing deployment scripts in your project
Does not modify GitHub repository settings automatically
Cannot approve releases without human authorization
Monitoring requires configured health check endpoints

useCases.title: Standard Release Orchestration
useCases.description: Automate major, minor, and patch releases with full testing, staging validation, and production deployment.

useCases.title: Hotfix Emergency Response
useCases.description: Fast-track critical production fixes with expedited approval process and immediate deployment.

useCases.title: Rollback Management
useCases.description: Monitor deployments and automatically trigger rollback when health metrics exceed thresholds.

promptTemplates.title: Standard Release
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the GitHub release management skill to release version 2.1.0. Run through the standard release workflow with staging validation and production deployment.

promptTemplates.title: Hotfix Release
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the GitHub release management skill to create a hotfix for a critical production bug. Fast-track the approval and deploy immediately.

promptTemplates.title: Rolling Deployment
promptTemplates.prompt: Deploy the new release using a rolling strategy. Monitor health metrics and be ready to rollback if error rate exceeds 5 percent.

promptTemplates.title: Canary Rollout
promptTemplates.prompt: Deploy the release using canary strategy at 10 percent traffic. Gradually increase to 25 percent, then 50 percent, then full rollout.

bestPractices: Run staging validation before any production deployment to catch 80 percent of issues early
Use canary or rolling deployments for major releases to minimize blast radius
Always test rollback procedures before production deployment
antiPatterns: Skipping validation tests to meet release deadlines
Deploying without monitoring and health checks configured
Releasing without changelog or release notes documentation

faq.question: What AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Claude Code, and Codex. It uses MCP tools from claude-flow for agent coordination.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy to any hosting platform?
faq.answer: No. The skill orchestrates agents but requires your own deployment scripts. The deploy.sh script handles platform-specific deployment.

faq.question: How does rollback work?
faq.answer: The skill monitors health metrics during deployment. If error rate exceeds thresholds, it triggers rollback to the previous version using rollback.sh.

faq.question: Is my repository data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only coordinates agents that follow your existing workflows. No data is sent to external services beyond your configured deployment targets.

faq.question: What if tests fail during release?
faq.answer: Any validation test failure blocks the release. The skill documents the failure and waits for human investigation before retry or abort.

faq.question: How is this different from GitHub Actions?
faq.answer: This skill coordinates AI agents that can make decisions and adapt workflows. GitHub Actions runs predefined scripts. They can work together.
